titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Johanna Drucker papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 13, 2019 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains writings, artwork, production files, business papers, printed material, and other papers by or relating to author, book artist, visual theorist, and cultural critic Johanna Drucker. It includes material documenting the production of works such as Against Fiction (1983), Damaged Spring (2003), Emerging Sentience (2001), Narratology (1994), Nova Reperta (2000), and other projects.

bioghist.head: Johanna Drucker
bioghist.p: Johanna Drucker (born May 30, 1952, Philadelphia) is a Distinguised Professor in the Department of Information Studies at the University of California, Los Angeles. She received her Ph.D and M.A. from University of California, Berkeley, and a B.F.A. from California College of Arts and Crafts. She is internationally known as a book artist, and for her work in the history of graphic design, typography, experimental poetry, fine art, and digital humanities.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Johanna Drucker Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Johanna Drucker Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.
Box 24 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The physical organization of the material, as determined by Johanna Drucker, has been preserved. Files relating to specific projects have not been collocated into new, discrete groupings.
The intellectual order of the collection differs from its physical arrangement. Files are listed alphabetically by project name.

did.unittitle: AC-DC Blues Gay Jazz Reissues Volume 1

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Against Culture: original drawings, texts, and layouts
Against Fiction: drawings and paste ups
Combo Meals: texts and design elements
Combo Meals: dummies, test, and image sources
Combo Meals: designs, tests, and images
The Current Line: texts, designs, mock ups
The Current Line: laser printed text
Damaged Nature/ Salvage Culture: print and design tests
Damaged Spring: mock ups, drawings, and dummies
Damaged Spring: linoleum cuts used for printing
Damaged Spring: linoleum cut printing blocks
Dark Decade: manuscripts, correspondence, hand painted cover design, and other material
Dark Decade: hand painted cover design
Deterring Discourse: printed sheets and imposition mock up
Johanna Drucker exhibition invitations and ephemera
Johanna Drucker exhibit poster and Ted Hughes poster
Eat, The Girl Who Did Nearly Nothing At All, Fruit and Vegetable Book, Tomato's Rescue: Xerox copies
Emerging Sentience: production notes, dummies, layout, and printed copy
Emerging Sentience: artist's statement and notes on exhibit
Embedment: proofs and texts of published piece
From A to Z: unbound sheets
Fabulas Feminae: manuscript notes and texts
Fabulas Feminae: notes and design tests
Fabulas Feminae: full finished output and digital prints
A Girl's Life: design sources, trials, correspondence, and inks
A Girl's Life: manuscript versions, mock ups, and ideas
Heavy Breathing: print dummy and paper test
Heavy Breathing: lead letter rubbing test
History of the/my Wor(l)d: hand painted cover design, mock ups, typescripts, and notes
History of the/my Wor(l)d: mock ups, cover design, and proof sheets
History of the/my Wor(l)d: paste-up parts, proof sheets, and hand painted cover design
History of the/my Wor(l)d: photographs, notes, and publicity material
Light to Fire: letterpress and linocut
Narratology: development of layout, structure, and text organization
Narratology: neon day-glo design mock ups
Night Crawlers: mock ups, drawings, and cover experiments
Nova Reperta: "Old Strad" texts, notes, development, trace layouts, and photos
Nova Reperta: plating and printing schedule
Nova Reperta: reduced scale and printing instructions
Nova Reperta: production and exhibit material, slides, and photographs
Nova Reperta: transfer printing and colophon, Joe Scheer correspondence
Otherspace/ Martians: correspondence, photographic prints, hand-drawn cover page, production dummy, and printed pages
Particles: page proofs, notes, and binding plan
Passionate Electron: drawings, texts, and correspondence
Passionate Electron: drawings, texts, and notes
Prove Before Laying: gift list and figurings of text
Prove Before Laying: texts, layouts, and hand drawn dummy
Prove Before Laying: production sheets, proofs, binding, dummy, and letters cut from font
Public Space: texts, layout, and mock ups
Pupa Proofs: black and white linocut, hand colored design, and text
Quantum: black ink and color paper tests
Romance Writer: mock up, workings for Abacus, and miscellany for Boxkite
Simulant Portrait: cost estimates, text typescript, and paste-up parts
Simulant Portrait: hand-painted designs and mock ups
Simulant Portrait: paste-up parts and texts
Stochastic: binding dummy with metal covers
Stochastic: cover etchings, film negatives, proof sheets, and marbled cover
Tabloid Life: designs, texts for Generator
Testament: design proofs and linocut prints
Testament of Women: drawings, designs, notes
Through Light and Alpha: original typed manuscript
Treatiste on Procedural Method: 3 title page proofs
VisCults: print out of published text and design
Woman Uses Language: copies of finished page for M/E/A/N/I/N/G
Word Made Flesh: typescript and other material
Word Made Flesh: book cover mock up
